content:
- Updated the bug task file to done/true with the acceptance checklist checked, then ran `.ralph/task_switch.sh` so Ralph advanced task tracking and sent its completion email.
- Next: inspect the final git status, commit all tracked changes with the required task-finished message including evidence from manual verification and check/test/lint, then push.

- Finished the stopped-run results bug end-to-end and pushed commit cc408df to master.
- Added a RED coordinator results test, fixed benchmarkrun result composition to hide only successful-stop `context canceled`, manually reverified the Docker Compose reproduction, and passed `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test`.
- Ralph task metadata is updated, task_switch was run, and the next context should follow the newly selected task from Ralph rather than returning to this bug.

- Read prior progress and confirmed the previous run finished the stopped-run-results bug and already switched Ralph onward.
- Inspected the active/done task summaries plus repo shape; actual stack is a Go module with internal packages for config, database, benchmark execution, coordinator/results, HTTP server, and Docker Compose/Kubernetes delivery examples.
- Next: refresh Ralph summaries, confirm chooser rules against blocker state, then write the selected task path into .ralph/current_task.txt and quit.

- Refreshed Ralph task summaries and re-read the chooser rules.
- Confirmed there are no active failing bugs; the only active failing task is `.ralph/tasks/story-99-manual-verify-everything/task-01-manual-verify-everything.md`, and its blocker `.ralph/tasks/story-09-ui/task-01-standalone-html-control-page.md` already passes true in `.ralph/current_tasks_done.md`.
- Next: continue from the story-99 manual verification task in `.ralph/current_task.txt`; it is the highest-precedence unblocked failing task.

- Read prior codex progress, current task pointer, and the required skill docs for improve-code-boundaries and tdd.
- Confirmed the active task is .ralph/tasks/story-99-manual-verify-everything/task-01-manual-verify-everything.md.
- Next: read the task file itself and determine whether this turn is a planning pass or an execution pass.

- Read the story-99 manual verification task file.
- Confirmed this task is a non-code manual verification exception: it explicitly forbids TDD and forbids running make test/check/lint during this task.
- Next: inspect repo surfaces needed for live verification (docs, compose, k8s, HTML page, API entrypoints) and start verifying features one by one until a real bug is found or the checklist is completed.

- Verified the local environment has Docker, Docker Compose, kubectl, and kind installed.
- Noted there is no browser binary installed; standalone HTML page verification will need an alternate live method later in the pass.
- Next: start the shipped Docker Compose stack and manually probe the documented health, readiness, benchmark, results, and metrics endpoints against the real service.

- Brought up the real Docker Compose stack successfully.
- Manually verified /healthz, /readyz, /benchmark, /benchmark/results, and /metrics on the live service; health/readiness are OK, idle state/results serialize, and pg_gobench_ metrics are exposed.
- Next: manually exercise start/alter/stop plus live state and results to verify schema setup, workload execution, stats, and single-active-run rejection.

- Manually started, altered, observed, and stopped a real benchmark run through the Compose stack.
- Verified single-active-run rejection and live stats/metrics updates; then inspected PostgreSQL and found benchmark tables under bench.* instead of the task-expected pg_gobench schema.
- Next: confirm whether that schema mismatch is an actual product contract bug or just stale task text, then create an add-bug task and stop this manual verification pass if it is real.

- Confirmed the live schema mismatch is distinct from the already-completed reserved-prefix startup bug: the service now runs, but only by violating the shipped pg_gobench schema contract and completed task acceptance.
- Per the manual-verification task, unrelated verification must stop at the first real bug and a new bug task must be created immediately.
- Next: use the add-bug workflow to write a reproducible bug task for the schema-contract mismatch, then run task switching so Ralph selects the bug before any more manual verification continues.

- Created .ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-benchmark-schema-contract-drifted-to-bench.md with real Compose and PostgreSQL reproduction steps.
- Stopped the manual verification pass at the first real bug, as required.
- Next: let Ralph switch to the new bug task and solve the schema-contract drift before returning to story-99 manual verification.
